name: Waitangi Day
traditions: Treaty commemorations; Māori cultural performances; waka (war canoe) ceremonies; hāngi (earth oven feast); official speeches.
historicalBackground: Waitangi Day marks the signing of the Treaty of Waitangi in 1840 between Māori chiefs and the British Crown, establishing New Zealand.
description: New Zealand's national day commemorating the founding treaty between Māori and the Crown, celebrated with cultural performances and reflection.
